solutions.solution: 'extra' to come to this conclusion. Now ask yourself this question: "Is this assumption universally true?" or "Can it never be false?" If you get 'yes' to the first question and 'no, never' for the second, accept it as definitely true. Otherwise, pick 'Definitely False or Probably False?'. For instance, look at Ex 29. If you think that the inference definitely true then you will have to make an extra assumption: "Failing to pay attention has been focussed on street-lighting because of accidents." But can it never be false? In other words, can it never be true that in many other previous cases the reason for attention-focussing was not an accident? Yes, can immediately see that it is improbable but not impossible.
